rag_no_rag_summary: "I'm sorry to hear about the damage to your Galaxy Watch6. Based on the information provided, a cracked screen after 14 months is generally not covered under the standard manufacturer's warranty, as it is outside the typical warranty period and physical damage is not covered. We recommend visiting an authorized Samsung Service Center, where our technicians can inspect the device and provide a repair estimate. If you have an extended protection plan or accidental damage coverage, please let us know so we can help verify your eligibility."
rag_grounded_summary: Thank you for contacting Samsung Support. Based on the information provided, your Galaxy Watch6 is 14 months from the date of purchase, which is outside the 12-month Standard Limited Warranty period. Additionally, a cracked screen is considered accidental damage and is covered only if you have an active Samsung Care+ plan. If you do not have active Care+ coverage, the repair would be a paid, out-of-pocket service. We recommend visiting or contacting a Samsung Authorized Service Center to receive a repair assessment and cost estimate. If you'd like, we can also help you check whether your device has an active Samsung Care+ plan.
stretch_notes: I created a conflict scenario where the model's assumed prior knowledge differed from the retrieved policy. I asked whether a Galaxy Watch6 with a cracked screen after 14 months would be covered, while the retrieved document clearly stated that the Standard Limited Warranty lasts 12 months and accidental damage is covered only by an active Samsung Care+ plan. The model followed the retrieved document and correctly answered that the device was not covered under the standard warranty (unless an active Care+ plan existed). This showed that the RAG document took precedence over conflicting prior knowledge.
